mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output that translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary periods or fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate surpasses 2.5%, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of high-inflation trigger months or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 45% of cases or shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output that systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with forward equity returns or positive return frequency drops below 50% during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising consumer prices creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months while CPIAUCSL rises
failure_condition: The dual signal fails to predict negative returns in over 60% of triggered instances or loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.
